summary: Three pins surface two problem families: a concrete operational gap (installed Rust binary drifting from source after cli.rs edits) and a strategic failure (V2 widget shipped a plugin contribution-point framework when three concrete tools were requested, compounding across multiple 'not useful' signals instead of pivoting on the first). The cargo/cli.rs issue is a concrete Rust instance of the named binary-rebuild mistake pattern. The V2 story is the highest-signal pin: it names a moment where an 'architecturally professional' choice diverged from the user's grounded need, and the agent repeatedly added surface rather than questioning the premise.

insights.instruction: Watch for this in future events: after any Edit targeting src/cli.rs, flag that the installed binary is now stale and cargo install must run before claiming the CLI change is live.

When a user says 'not useful' after a feature ship, do not add more surface. Stop and audit whether the architectural premise matches the user's actual grounded need. Concrete user requests are not prompts to generalize into a framework.

summary: Three pins from 2026-05-29 split across two problem surfaces: a catastrophic compound failure in the Versable frontend agent session exposing a performative-self-criticism anti-pattern and a hinter pipeline gap, and two structural bugs in i-dream itself where both the daily digest and the interactive audit generate fresh data instead of reading authoritative persisted state. The Versable pin is the most complex — it names five known live slugs that still failed to correct in-session behavior, suggesting enforcement surface (UserPromptSubmit) is insufficient. The i-dream pins share a root cause worth associating: consumers that silently discard persisted state in favor of re-generation.

A single high-signal pin describes a cross-cutting false-positive class in raw-command-string-scanning hooks: guards that match dangerous tokens anywhere in a shell invocation string will trip on documentation prose, RCA content, and here-doc bodies that discuss the very commands being blocked. The cli-gating subsystem already solved this (quoted-string awareness + 150 tests), but safe-delete and guard-system-dir-writes have not adopted the fix. The actionable dream output is a refactoring proposal and a shared helper extraction to unify all five scanning hooks under the same command-position-aware parser.

insights.instruction: Extract a shared `is_token_in_command_position(token, argv)` helper — reusing cli-gating's existing quoted-string and heredoc-body parsing logic — and thread it through safe-delete.sh, guard-system-dir-writes.sh, comment-hygiene, and env-access hooks. ci-gating's 150-test suite becomes the regression harness for all five.
When reviewing any new guard/hook proposal, apply the same context-awareness bar as for ADR exceptions: ask 'does this matcher understand the grammatical position of the token, or only its presence?'

Add a hook-authoring checklist item to PLACEMENT.md or scripts/hooks/README.md: 'Does this hook scan raw command strings? If yes, does it use the shared command-position parser? If no shared parser exists yet, implement one in lib/ and adopt it here.'

summary: Two pins from 2026-06-19 capture the graduation arc of the ai-smell-prose-against-stored-voice S3 atone event (mist-20260619-194539-c9) into rules/audience-aware-writing.md, which is now live in CLAUDE.md Tier-0. The graduation rule side is complete; the mechanical enforcement side — Stop-stage prose hook prop-20260619-194931-ba — remains status:open. Historical precedent from source-comment-hygiene (same class of advisory rule, same account) and the Versable hinter-failure session (pin-20260529152121-e6) both point toward the same conclusion: the advisory rule alone will not correct the pattern under pressure.

A single pin from 2026-06-21 grounds itself in a concrete ARG_MAX bug caught by immediately exercising a new vision command against real data the instant the model finished downloading. The latent theme is not the bug itself but its cause: the user's 'reliability over performance' evaluation axis drove the immediate exercise and surfaced what static inspection would have missed. This preference refines and unifies three existing patterns — exercise-based-verification, structure-over-one-shotting, efficacy-over-speed — into an explicit trust hierarchy that should graduate into global memory.

insights.instruction: When any local-models bin/ tool processes image files or large text blobs, test it with a real-sized input before declaring done. Shell arg limits are silent — no warning, just truncation or ENOMEM. Pattern to watch: jq --arg combined with binary-to-base64 output or large model responses.
Audit bin/probe and any new bin/ commands added to local-models for the large-buffer-through-shell-args pattern. If found, apply the Python-inline or stdin-pipe pattern used in bin/see:82-103.

summary: The two new pins form a paired case study in the same failure class. The first (pin-20260624080929-6d) documents a 3rd S3 recurrence of speculative-abstractions despite a live rule, atone hints, and previous corrections, plus a double-recording slip from misreading background CLI silence as process death. The second (pin-20260624205747-54) shows the structural inverse: a design → adversarial-skeptic → cheap empirical test loop killing the same class of over-build before any code was written, with the three relevant atone slugs used as a predictive checklist rather than an after-the-fact guardrail. Together they reveal that slugs are diagnostic at design time but not corrective mid-session, and that an empirical test is what converts skeptic opinion into falsifiable fact.
